channel.title: Who am I on xow
channel.description: Recent content in Who am I on xow

item.title: Complete tutorial on deploying Django app on Ubuntu at digitalocean + SSL certificate https
item.description: [TOC] It can be quite frustrating/challenging for Django newbies to deploy their app because a bunch of new concepts should be known in order to play the game with confidence. This tutorial gives you all the basics and further reference. The structure of the project can be seen in the following screenshot: the project my_blog is itself located in the blog folder. Deployment in a nutshell You need a web server often Nginx or apache to handle http requests an application server called gunicorn to serve your django app a django app a database management system often mysql or postgresql to store information.

item.title: Independent t-test by hand in Python: with equal sample sizes and variance
item.description: Import necessary packages import numpy as np from scipy import stats from numpy.random import seed Generate data # set the seed seed(1) N = 10 # Gaussian distributed data with mean = 4 and var = 1 # see https://docs.scipy.org/doc/numpy-1.15.1/reference/generated/numpy.random.randn.html a = np.random.randn(N) + 4 # Gaussian distributed data with with mean = 0 and var = 1 b = np.random.randn(N) print("The mean of a is around 4 ->",np.mean(a),"\nThe mean of b is around 0 ->",np.

item.title: Major word vector parameters in Gensim
item.description: The skip-gram model. From Lilian Weng import logging import multiprocessing from gensim.models import Word2Vec cores = multiprocessing.cpu_count() w2v_model = Word2Vec(min_count=5, # window=5, size=100, # sample=6e-5, # alpha=0.03, # min_alpha=0.0007, # negative=20, sg=1, workers=cores-1) See here and here for more explanations vector_size: number of dimensions of the embeddings, default 100. For comparaison you can set it to 200 and 300. Some set it even to 1000. window: maximum distance between a target word and words around the target word.

item.title: Type check in Python using mypy
item.description: Problem Create a python script named test.py. def hello_name(name: str) -> int: return "hehe" hello_name(3) Two things are wrong in the above code. a str is expected as parameter a int is expected as output However the function will still work without any error report. This kind of bug is the most difficult to resolve. Solution pip install mypy then in the terminal, run mypy test.py Here is the output which debugs the code for you.

item.title: Understand the difference between __repr__ and __str__ in Python
item.description: (1) Suppose that you have created a class class Person: def __init__(self, name, age): self.name = name self.age = age (2) And initialized an instance p1 = Person("John", 36) print(p1) An uninstructive output: <main.Animal object at 0x7f9060250410> (3) __str__ changes the behavior of print, __repr__ makes print unnecessary class Animal: def __init__(self, color, age, breed): self.color = color self.age = age self.breed = breed def __str__(self): return f"{self.

item.title: Complete tutorial on scraping French news from Le Monde
item.description: Author: Xiaoou Wang, Master’s student (currently in Paris) in NLP looking for a phd position/contrat cifre. Blog/Linkedin/Email Why you need web scraping and why I wrote this tutorial Sooner or later in your career you will find yourself in a situation where you need to build your own corpus for common nlp tests. Some days ago I wrote a tutorial on scraping news on the French journal le monde.

item.title: I created/share two AlfredWorkflow plugins to open folders/files in Pycharm/Rstudio
item.description: Author: Xiaoou Wang, Master’s student (currently in Paris) in NLP looking for a phd position/contrat cifre. Blog/Linkedin/Email Two Alfred Plugins pycharmAlfredWorkflow and rstudioAlfredWorkflow are two alfred workflow plugins that I make to quickly open files/folders in Pycharm/Rstudio. py, pyf, open a folder with Pycharm. rs, rsf, open a folder with Rstudio. Instructions Using the keyword py, search for only folders within your home folder.

item.title: Undefined and control flow in Javascript (why do you see undefined when typing let x = 3 at console)
item.description: Author: Xiaoou Wang, Master’s student (currently in Paris) in NLP looking for a phd position/contrat cifre. Blog/Linkedin/Email Question When you type let x = 3 (declare a variable) in a console of Chrome/Firefox/… often you get a mysterious undefined. It’s the same story when you type function f(){} (function declaration). The explanation can be quite intimidating as this answer here. Here is a simple (well not sooo simple) answer.

item.title: About
item.description: Hugo is the world’s fastest framework for building websites. It is written in Go. It makes use of a variety of open source projects including: https://github.com/russross/blackfriday https://github.com/alecthomas/chroma https://github.com/muesli/smartcrop https://github.com/spf13/cobra https://github.com/spf13/viper Learn more and contribute on GitHub.
